parsed.answer: The question contains a false premise. Ireland is not part of England. The Republic of Ireland is a sovereign, independent nation (independent since 1922, republic since 1949), a member of the European Union with its own government, parliament, and currency. Northern Ireland is part of the United Kingdom, but the UK and England are not the same thing — England is one of four constituent countries within the UK. Historically, Ireland was under British rule for centuries, but that ended over 100 years ago.
